title: Brasileirão loader reads away team from the away_team_state column
evidence: loader.go:48 sets AwayTeam from row[4]; header is datetime,home_team,home_team_state,away_team(3),away_team_state(4),... so row[4] is the 2-letter state. Sample: 'Palmeiras-SP,SP,Portuguesa-SP,SP,1,1' -> AwayTeam becomes 'SP'. All 4,180 Brasileirão away teams are corrupted to state codes, breaking away-team search, head-to-head, standings and team stats for the flagship dataset.
suggestion: Read AwayTeam from row[3] (away_team), not row[4].

title: TeamStats win/draw/loss classification is case-sensitive but lookup is case-insensitive
evidence: store.go:84 finds matches via lowercase index key, but store.go:101-107 compares m.HomeTeam == team / m.AwayTeam == team case-sensitively. A caller passing 'flamengo' (any non-exact case) still gets Matches>0 from the index, yet every match falls through to the else branch (store.go:110) and is counted as a Loss, so Wins=Draws=0 and WinRate=0. Only exact-case inputs (as in the tests) are correct.
suggestion: Use strings.EqualFold(m.HomeTeam, team) / EqualFold(m.AwayTeam, team) for the W/D/L branches, consistent with the case-insensitive index.

title: Query inputs are not normalized for state suffix, so suffixed team queries miss
evidence: store.go:43 and store.go:80 lowercase the raw input but stored team names were normalized (state suffix stripped) in loader.go:47. Querying 'Palmeiras-SP' (key 'palmeiras-sp') never matches the stored 'Palmeiras', returning zero matches/stats even though the spec (TASK.md:302-308) requires normalizing name variations for matching.
suggestion: Apply normalizeTeamName() to the query term before the index lookup in SearchMatches, TeamStats and HeadToHead.

title: Human-readable formatters are never wired into tool handlers
evidence: tools.go:352 FormatTextContent and the helpers formatPlayerList/formatStats/formatBigWins are unused; the handlers in main.go:66-160 return only structured output and never call them, so the formatting/'properly formatted responses' work is dead code.
suggestion: Attach the formatted text as CallToolResult content in each handler, or remove the unused formatters.

title: Empty package directories loader/ store/ tools/ left in the workspace
evidence: loader/, store/ and tools/ are empty directories alongside the flat loader.go/store.go/tools.go files; they appear to be an abandoned package layout.
